title: License-segregated bundle builder + CI fail-on-violation assertion
context: Bundle assembly is where license mislabeling causes real harm: a non-commercial article in a commercial bundle is a compliance failure, not a minor defect. This task implements the bundle builder that assembles license-segregated output directories strictly keyed on licenseTier, emits per-bundle LICENSE manifests, and ships a CI assertion that fails the build if any article lands in a disallowed bundle. A deliberate fixture mis-routing test is required so the fail-on-violation path is continuously exercised. Depends on license-004 (resolver output) and gate-002 (tiering rules).
objective: Implement the license-segregated bundle builder: assemble redistributable bundles strictly by licenseTier, emit per-bundle LICENSE manifests, and ship a CI assertion that fails the build when a mis-tiered article is detected — with a deliberate fixture that proves the failure path works.
acceptanceCriteria: Builds bundles strictly keyed on licenseTier (commercial, non-commercial, no-derivatives, metadata-only); no article is placed in a bundle of a disallowed tier.
Emits a per-bundle LICENSE manifest listing each included article's PMCID, license id, license URL, and tier; no article's license is modified or relicensed.

Ships a fixture corpus where a non-commercial article is deliberately mis-routed to the commercial bundle and the build fails in CI with a clear error message; the fail-on-violation path is always exercised in CI.
Ships a fixture where an article with a flagged patient-content signal is mis-routed to a redistributable bundle and the build likewise fails.
Build output is deterministic: given the same input manifest and articles, the bundle contents and per-bundle manifest are byte-identical across runs.
Records the manifest content hash (SHA-256 of the manifest JSON) so independent rebuilds can verify reproducibility.
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; commit is DCO signed-off; code MIT licensed.
resources: pmc-cancer-gate-002 (tiering rules defining allowed/disallowed bundle membership)
PLAN.md (Solution approach — Step 7: Bundle build; Test fixtures section)
output: A merged PR adding the bundle builder module (TypeScript/ESM), per-bundle LICENSE manifest emitter, CI fail-on-violation assertion, and fixture corpora (mis-routed non-commercial + mis-routed patient-content) that confirm the failure path in CI.
